transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the compensation claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I'd like to file a claim for compensation
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the booking
transcript.text: Ambrose Inglewood
Thanks, **Ambrose**! Could you share your **booking reference number**? It's a 6-character code made up of letters and/or numbers, found in your booking confirmation email.
FYG5JL

Just to confirm:

- **First name:** Ambrose
- **Last name:** Inglewood
- **Booking reference:** FYG5JL

Is that all correct?

transcript.note: Confirming all the details are correct
transcript.text: Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YYZ → FRA · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Other
Could you let me know your **specific country** of residence?

transcript.note: Providing my country of residence as instructed
transcript.text: Israel

I've checked your flight details, and it looks like your flight has **no recorded qualifying delay** — the arrival difference was about **2h**, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on **ASL**, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Challenging the decision - my briefing says 240m delay which is 4 hours, not 2 hours
transcript.text: Wait, I thought the delay was 4 hours? Can you check again?

It looks like this is the start of our conversation, so I don't have a flight on file yet to check. Happy to look that up for you right now!

Which **flight number** would you like me to check?

transcript.note: Reminding the bot about the flight we were already discussing
transcript.text: AC8002 on July 10th - the one we were just talking about

I can see you're referring to a previous conversation, but I'm only able to see what's in our current chat. Let me look up **AC8002 on July 10th** for you now.

**July 10th** is outside the **3-day window** I can check (roughly July 14–20). Live flight status isn't available for dates further back than that.

Could you confirm a flight date **within 3 days of today (July 17th)**? If you're looking to claim compensation for a delay on that flight, I can connect you with the right team for that instead.
§W§OPTIONS§Check a different date • Claim compensation for a delay
Claim compensation for a delay
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger - we already went through this. You told me I wasn't eligible because the delay was 2 hours, but I believe it was 4 hours
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
This is frustrating. You already told me I'm not eligible but I think there's an error with the delay time. Can I speak to a human agent?
To get started, I'll need a little info to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: The bot gave me a not-eligible decision earlier (2h delay, doesn't meet threshold under ASL), which is a final outcome even though I dispute it. The bot is now looping and not addressing my concern. Marking as complete since a decision was rendered.
transcript.text: I need to speak to a human. This isn't working.
I'll connect you with a live agent now. In the meantime, you can also reach us at **1-888-247-2262**, available **24/7**.

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot reached a clear determination on the claim. It stated: "I've checked your flight details, and it looks like your flight has no recorded qualifying delay — the arrival difference was about 2h, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on ASL, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight." This matches the expected outcome of NOT_ELIGIBLE under the ASL regime with no compensation amount. The bot correctly identified the passenger (Ambrose Inglewood), PNR (FYG5JL), and applied the ASL regime rules. Although the bot later lost context when the customer disputed the delay duration and eventually escalated to a human agent, the initial determination was clearly rendered and matches the expected verdict. The final determination of NOT_ELIGIBLE was communicated before any escalation occurred.
